title: Emperor Go-Suzaku

infobox.value: 7 February 1045(1045-02-07) (aged 35) Higashi-sanjō Tei (東三条第), Heian Kyō (Kyōto)
[Teishi](/wiki/Princess_Teishi "Princess Teishi")  ​ (m. 1027)​      [Fujiwara no Genshi](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Genshi "Fujiwara no Genshi")  ​  ​ (m. 1037; died 1039)​

* [Emperor Go-Reizei](/wiki/Emperor_Go-Reizei "Emperor Go-Reizei") * Princess Ryōshi * Princess Kenshi * [Emperor Go-Sanjō](/wiki/Emperor_Go-Sanj%C5%8D "Emperor Go-Sanjō") * Princess Yūshi * Princess Baishi * Princess Seishi
[Imperial House of Japan](/wiki/Imperial_House_of_Japan "Imperial House of Japan")

(後朱雀天皇, *Go-Suzaku-[tennō](/wiki/Tenn%C5%8D "Tennō")*; December 14, 1009 – February 7, 1045) was the 69th [emperor](/wiki/Emperor "Emperor") of [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"),[[1]](#cite_note-kunaicho-1) according to the traditional [order of succession](/wiki/List_of_Emperors_of_Japan "List of Emperors of Japan").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

(*[Chōgen](/wiki/Ch%C5%8Dgen "Chōgen") 9, 17th day of the 4th month*) : In the 9th year of Emperor Go-Ichijō's reign (後一条天皇九年), he died; and the succession (‘‘senso’’) was received by his younger brother.[[8]](#cite_note-8)
(*[Chōgen](/wiki/Ch%C5%8Dgen "Chōgen") 9, 7th month*): Emperor Go-Suzaku is said to have acceded to the throne (‘‘sokui’’).[[9]](#cite_note-9)
(*[Kantoku](/wiki/Kantoku "Kantoku") 2, 16th day of the 1st month*): Emperor Go-Suzaku abdicated.[[7]](#cite_note-b311-7)

(*[Kantoku](/wiki/Kantoku "Kantoku") 2, 18th day of the 1st month*): The former-Emperor Go-Suzaku ordained as a Buddhist monk and died the same day at the age of 37.[[10]](#cite_note-10) His reign has lasted nine years—five in the *nengō* *Chōryaku*, four in *Chōkyu*, and 2 in *Kantoku*.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Emperor of Japan from 1036 to 1045

| [House](/wiki/Dynasty "Dynasty") | [Imperial House of Japan](/wiki/Imperial_House_of_Japan "Imperial House of Japan") |

**Emperor Go-Suzaku** (後朱雀天皇, *Go-Suzaku-[tennō](/wiki/Tenn%C5%8D "Tennō")*; December 14, 1009 – February 7, 1045) was the 69th [emperor](/wiki/Emperor "Emperor") of [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"),[[1]](#cite_note-kunaicho-1) according to the traditional [order of succession](/wiki/List_of_Emperors_of_Japan "List of Emperors of Japan").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Go-Suzaku's reign spanned the years from [1036 through 1045](/wiki/Heian_period "Heian period").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

This 11th-century sovereign was named after the 10th-century [Emperor Suzaku](/wiki/Emperor_Suzaku "Emperor Suzaku") and *go-* (後), translates literally as "later;" and thus, he is sometimes called the "Later Emperor Suzaku". The Japanese word "*go*" has also been translated to mean the "second one;" and in some older sources, this emperor may be identified as "Suzaku, the second" or as "Suzaku II."

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Emperor_Go-Suzaku&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Before his ascension to the [Chrysanthemum Throne](/wiki/Chrysanthemum_Throne "Chrysanthemum Throne"), his personal name (his *imina*)[[4]](#cite_note-4) was Atsunaga*-shinnō* (敦良親王).[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

His father was [Emperor Ichijō](/wiki/Emperor_Ichijo "Emperor Ichijo"). His mother was [Fujiwara](/wiki/Fujiwara_clan "Fujiwara clan") no Akiko/[Shōshi](/wiki/Empress_Sh%C5%8Dshi "Empress Shōshi") (藤原彰子), the daughter of [Fujiwara no Michinaga](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Michinaga "Fujiwara no Michinaga") (藤原道長). He was the younger brother and heir to [Emperor Go-Ichijō](/wiki/Emperor_Go-Ichijo "Emperor Go-Ichijo").

Go-Suzaku had five Empresses and seven Imperial children.[[7]](#cite_note-b311-7)

## Events of Go-Suzaku's life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Emperor_Go-Suzaku&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Events of Go-Suzaku's life")]

* **May 15, 1036** (*[Chōgen](/wiki/Ch%C5%8Dgen "Chōgen") 9, 17th day of the 4th month*) : In the 9th year of Emperor Go-Ichijō's reign (後一条天皇九年), he died; and the succession (‘‘senso’’) was received by his younger brother.[[8]](#cite_note-8)
* **1036** (*[Chōgen](/wiki/Ch%C5%8Dgen "Chōgen") 9, 7th month*): Emperor Go-Suzaku is said to have acceded to the throne (‘‘sokui’’).[[9]](#cite_note-9)
* **February 5, 1045** (*[Kantoku](/wiki/Kantoku "Kantoku") 2, 16th day of the 1st month*): Emperor Go-Suzaku abdicated.[[7]](#cite_note-b311-7)
* **February 7, 1045** (*[Kantoku](/wiki/Kantoku "Kantoku") 2, 18th day of the 1st month*): The former-Emperor Go-Suzaku ordained as a Buddhist monk and died the same day at the age of 37.[[10]](#cite_note-10) His reign has lasted nine years—five in the *nengō* *Chōryaku*, four in *Chōkyu*, and 2 in *Kantoku*.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/03/Go-shichi_no_kiri_crest_2.svg/250px-Go-shichi_no_kiri_crest_2.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Go-shichi_no_kiri_crest_2.svg)

Decorative emblems (*kiri*) of the Hosokawa clan are found at [Ryōan-ji](/wiki/Ry%C5%8Dan-ji "Ryōan-ji"). Go-Suzaku is amongst six other emperors entombed near what had been the residence of [Hosokawa Katsumoto](/wiki/Hosokawa_Katsumoto "Hosokawa Katsumoto") before the [Ōnin War](/wiki/%C5%8Cnin_War "Ōnin War").

The actual site of Go-Suzaku's [grave](/wiki/Grave_(burial) "Grave (burial)") is unknown.[[1]](#cite_note-kunaicho-1) This emperor is traditionally venerated at a [memorial](/wiki/Memorial "Memorial") [Shinto](/wiki/Shinto "Shinto") [shrine](/wiki/Shrine "Shrine") (*misasagi*) at Kyoto.

The [Imperial Household Agency](/wiki/Imperial_Household_Agency "Imperial Household Agency") designates this location as Go-Suzaku's [mausoleum](/wiki/Mausoleum "Mausoleum"). It is formally named *Enjō-ji no misasagi*.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Go-Suzaku is buried amongst the "Seven Imperial Tombs" at Ryōan-ji Temple in Kyoto.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

The specific mound which commemorates the Hosokawa Emperor Go-Suzaku is today named *Shu-zan.*[[13]](#cite_note-moscher277-13)

The emperor's burial place would have been quite humble in the period after Go-Suzaku died.[[13]](#cite_note-moscher277-13)

These tombs reached their present state as a result of the 19th century restoration of imperial sepulchers (*misasagi*) which were ordered by Emperor Meiji.[[13]](#cite_note-moscher277-13)

The final resting place of Emperor Go-Suzaku's consort, Teishi Nai-shinnō (1013–1094), is here as well.[[13]](#cite_note-moscher277-13)

### Kugyō

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Emperor_Go-Suzaku&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Kugyō")]

*[Kugyō](/wiki/Kugy%C5%8D "Kugyō")* (公卿) is a collective term for the very few most powerful men attached to the court of the [Emperor of Japan](/wiki/Emperor_of_Japan "Emperor of Japan") in pre-[Meiji](/wiki/Meiji_period "Meiji period") eras. Even during those years in which the court's actual influence outside the palace walls was minimal, the hierarchic organization persisted.

In general, this elite group included only three to four men at a time. These were hereditary courtiers whose experience and background would have brought them to the pinnacle of a life's career. During Go-Suzaku's reign, this apex of the *[Daijō-kan](/wiki/Daij%C5%8D-kan "Daijō-kan")* included:

* *[Sadaijin](/wiki/Sadaijin "Sadaijin")*, Fujiwara Yorimichi, 992–1074.[[7]](#cite_note-b311-7)
* *[Udaijin](/wiki/Udaijin "Udaijin")*, Fujiwara Sanesuke, 957–1046.[[7]](#cite_note-b311-7)
* *[Nadaijin](/wiki/Nadaijin "Nadaijin")*, Fujiwara Norimichi, 997–1075.[[7]](#cite_note-b311-7)
* *[Dainagon](/wiki/Dainagon "Dainagon")*

## Eras of Go-Suzaku's reign

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Emperor_Go-Suzaku&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Eras of Go-Suzaku's reign")]

The years of Go-Suzaku's reign are more specifically identified by more than one [era name](/wiki/Japanese_era_names "Japanese era names") or *[nengō](/wiki/Neng%C5%8D "Nengō")*.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

* *[Chōgen](/wiki/Ch%C5%8Dgen "Chōgen")* (1028–1037)
* *[Chōryaku](/wiki/Ch%C5%8Dryaku "Chōryaku")* (1037–1040)
* *[Chōkyū](/wiki/Ch%C5%8Dky%C5%AB "Chōkyū")* (1040–1044)
* *[Kantoku](/wiki/Kantoku "Kantoku")* (1044–1046)

## Consorts and children

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Emperor_Go-Suzaku&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Consorts and children")]

* Crown Princess (died before Emperor's accession): [Fujiwara no Yoshiko](/w/index.php?title=Fujiwara_no_Kishi(Michinaga%27s_daughter)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Fujiwara no Kishi(Michinaga's daughter) (page does not exist)") [[ja](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%97%A4%E5%8E%9F%E5%AC%89%E5%AD%90 "ja:藤原嬉子")] (藤原嬉子; 1007-1025), [Fujiwara no Michinaga](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Michinaga "Fujiwara no Michinaga")‘s 6th daughter
  + First Son: Imperial Prince Chikahito (親仁親王) later [Emperor Go-Reizei](/wiki/Emperor_Go-Reizei "Emperor Go-Reizei")
* Empress (*Kōgō*): Imperial [Princess Teishi](/wiki/Princess_Teishi "Princess Teishi") (禎子内親王; 1013–1094) later Yōmeimon’in (陽明門院), [Emperor Sanjō](/wiki/Emperor_Sanj%C5%8D "Emperor Sanjō")‘s 3rd daughter
  + First Daughter: Imperial Princess Nagako/*Ryōshi* (良子内親王, 1029–1077) – [Saiō](/wiki/Sai%C5%8D "Saiō") at [Ise Shrine](/wiki/Ise_Shrine "Ise Shrine") 1036–1045 (Ippon-Jusangū, 一品准三宮)
  + Second daughter: Imperial Princess Yoshiko/*Kenshi* (娟子内親王, 1032–1103) – [Saiin](/wiki/Saiin_(Priestess) "Saiin (Priestess)") at [Kamo Shrine](/wiki/Kamo_Shrine "Kamo Shrine") 1036–1045, later married Minamoto Toshifusa
  + Second Son: Imperial Prince Takahito (尊仁親王) later [Emperor Go-Sanjo](/wiki/Emperor_Go-Sanjo "Emperor Go-Sanjo")
* Empress (*Chūgū*): [Fujiwara no Genshi](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Genshi "Fujiwara no Genshi") (藤原嫄子; 1016–1039), Imperial Prince Atsuyasu's daughter and [Fujiwara no Yorimichi](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Yorimichi "Fujiwara no Yorimichi")‘s adopted daughter
  + Third Daughter: Imperial Princess Sukeko/*Yūshi* (祐子内親王; 1038–1105) – (Sanpon-Jusangū, 三品准三宮)
  + Fourth Daughter: Imperial Princess Miwako/*Baishi* (禖子内親王; 1039–1096) (Rokujō Saiin, 六条斎院) – [Saiin](/wiki/Saiin_(Priestess) "Saiin (Priestess)") at [Kamo Shrine](/wiki/Kamo_Shrine "Kamo Shrine") 1046–1058
* Consort (*Nyōgo*): Fujiwara no Nariko/*Seishi* (藤原生子; 1014–1068), [Fujiwara no Norimichi](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Norimichi "Fujiwara no Norimichi")‘s eldest daughter
* Consort (*Nyōgo*): Fujiwara no Nobuko/*Enshi* (藤原延子; 1016–1095), [Fujiwara no Yorimune](/w/index.php?title=Fujiwara_no_Yorimune&action=edit&redlink=1 "Fujiwara no Yorimune (page does not exist)") [[ja](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%97%A4%E5%8E%9F%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%97 "ja:藤原頼宗")]‘s 2nd daughter
  + Fifth Daughter: Imperial Princess Masako/*Seishi* (正子内親王; 1045–1114) (Oshinokōji-Saiin, 押小路斎院) – [Saiin](/wiki/Saiin_(Priestess) "Saiin (Priestess)") at [Kamo Shrine](/wiki/Kamo_Shrine "Kamo Shrine") 1058–1069
